projectName: Canon Boundary Guard for GPT Projects
projectDescription: A source-bundle protocol and prompt-engineering frame that keeps ChatGPT Projects from silently promoting chat messages, assumptions, and drafts into accepted project truth.
keyFeatures: Provenance layering (L0–L3) that classifies evidence vs. conversation residue
Simulated PreToolUse gate at the semantic persistence boundary
Deterministic save labels: [SAFE TO SAVE], [DO NOT SAVE], [STATE DELTA], [DRAFT]
Session state tracking via SESSION_STATE.json with sequence numbers and active L0 sources
CANON_STATE_DELTA snapshots for durable, recoverable state hand-off
Optional Python validators: validate_state.py, artifact_fingerprint.py, extract_proof.py
Mechanical proof-of-read requirement before any persistent write
Read-only recovery mode when valid session state is unavailable
publicApiSurface: Source classes: L0, L1, L1A, L2, L3
Operating modes: Mode A, Mode B, Mode C
Save labels: [SAFE TO SAVE], [DO NOT SAVE - L1/L3 PRESENT], [STATE DELTA - SAVE/PASTE ONLY AS RECOVERY MATERIAL], [DRAFT - REQUIRES OPERATOR APPROVAL]
SESSION_STATE.json schema (protocol, protocol_version, state_seq, updated_at, active_l0_sources, authorized_deltas, open_conflicts, pending_decisions, last_persistent_artifacts)
CANON_STATE_DELTA schema (type, seq, timestamp, basis, decision, current_state, operator_action_required)
